story.from: bdo's carry-over brief, 2026-06-20 (strategy-metabolism.md §7): the explore lobe must not land alone — exploration creates an immediate need for claiming, or the system grows beautiful overgrowth. Claim is the bridge from a conjectural ScoutCTA to bounded work.
story.framing: The Scout lobe (landed, PR #321) emits one marked call to action. Left alone that is unmanaged possibility floating with no edge. The metabolism is Scout -> Claim -> Build -> Prune; Build is the existing loop, Prune is the next line. Claim is the missing bridge between possibility and execution: it gives a conjecture an edge so a session or bdo can pick it up — or so Prune can later retire it. It does NOT prove the CTA (proving is Build's job: gate -> stamp -> land); it only bounds it.
story.work: loop/claim.py (done-line 0158) — a read-only/propose-only fold that, given a ScoutCTA, FIRST validates it through loop/scout_cta.validate (it only ever bounds a real, grounded conjecture — an invalid/ungrounded one is REFUSED) and then produces one marked claimed_frontier per §7: status:claimed_frontier, source_cta, claim_type in {arc,epic,spike,branch,watch,defer} (derived from the move's own shape, not constant), owner, guard_policy, budget, review_date, exit_condition, expiry. Every claim is BOUNDED (non-empty exit_condition + expiry — an unbounded claim is the overgrowth this organ prevents) and never PROVES (no truth, never minted). tests/test_claim.py proves the teeth: a valid conjecture is bounded, an invalid/ungrounded one is refused, the claim never mints, and the claim_type is derived not constant.
story.need: bdo's confirm-arc on epic.strategy (given — adm.bc55d43121f8 on the trunk) authorizes the loop to carry this piece. Prune (§8, its build requisitions a panel review for intent — his flag, the weakest organ) is the next line. Nothing else owed: read-only, local, stdlib, reversible.
story.value: A Scout conjecture stops being a floating possibility: Claim gives it a typed, budgeted, expiring edge a session or bdo can act on — without proving it (that stays the Build loop's gated job). It is the bridge that keeps exploration from becoming overgrowth, and the half Prune later cleans up.
story.text: From bdo's metabolism (Scout -> Claim -> Build -> Prune), I need a read-only organ that turns a valid ScoutCTA into one bounded claimed_frontier — refusing to bound an unlawful conjecture and never proving it — because a Scout call to action with no edge is unmanaged possibility, and the system has no organ today that bounds a conjecture into workable, expiring intent.

risk.rationale: A pure read-only fold + a §10 test, stdlib, no network, no git, no log writes. Claim bounds; it never judges, proves, mints, or lands. Reversible by deletion. Prune and the wider metabolism stay later lines and bdo's.

briefing.headline: give a Scout conjecture an edge — bounded, not proven
briefing.value: Scout proposes a move; Claim turns it into a typed, budgeted, expiring unit of intent a session or you can pick up — without pretending it is proven. The bridge between possibility and execution.
briefing.why_now: The Scout lobe landed; a conjecture with no way to become bounded work is overgrowth. This is the next organ of the metabolism you named.
briefing.if_accepted: Scout -> Claim is whole; Build (the existing loop) carries owned claims through the pipeline; Prune (with its panel review for intent) rides next to retire dead frontiers.
briefing.if_rejected: Scout conjectures float with no edge — the overgrowth the metabolism was designed to avoid.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low: read-only, local, reversible by deletion; nothing lands, mints, or alters truth.
briefing.mechanism: loop/claim.py validates a ScoutCTA through the contract, derives a claim_type from the move's shape, and emits one bounded claimed_frontier (exit_condition + expiry), marked proposed; it refuses an unlawful conjecture and never proves. The cut and Prune stay yours (D-4).

source_artifacts: atom.scout-fold.v0 (the Scout lobe this bounds the output of)

_note: Birth record and content-hash source; never mutated (D-5). The Claim organ of epic.strategy (Scout -> Claim -> Build -> Prune). Read-only/propose-only; Prune is the next line, its build requisitioning a panel review for intent (strategy-metabolism.md §8).
